item.title: Something Is Happening
item.description: Building things sparks joy. Everyone I talk to is shaking with excitement. I'm not sure where this goes, but it sure is fun.

item.title: Are web apps really slower than native?
item.description:
TL;DR
Web apps aren't slower than native apps. The web platform makes the performant path harder to reach than it should be. Native platforms bake perform...

item.title: Don't let Claude Code read your secrets: why you need to set up sandboxing today
item.description: Claude Code is powerful. It reads your files, runs commands, and edits your codebase. But its default setup doesn't stop it from reading your secrets. Deny rules won't help in bypass mode. Native sandboxing will.

item.title: LLM coding is the wrong layer of abstraction
item.description: Conventional wisdom tells you that LLMs are good at automating the generation of boring, repetitive boilerplate code. I can agree, but why d...

item.title: Django Table, Filter and Export With Htmx | Fundor333
item.description: A little code fragment for show how to use in the same project Htmx, Django-table2 e Django-filters

item.title: Closing the Loop – Alexander Feldman's Blog
item.description: This quarter, 4,500 CEOs told PwC their AI investments produced nothing. Separately, someone used AI to rewrite SQLite in Rust — 2,000 times slower. I have a cunning plan: what if we used computers to do actual computing?

item.title: It was nice while it lasted
item.description: I got into software engineering because I was always fascinated by getting computers to do stuff. It's been a dream to earn a living doing something I would ...

item.title: How the Claude Mythos leak happened
Running out of Disk Space in Production
item.description: A tale about misconfiguring nginx, from adventures in production

item.title: Thoughts on the SaaSpocalypse
item.description: The multi dimensional threats of AI to SaaS businesses.

item.title: The Vibecoders Are Coming For Us
item.description: Oh man, the past few months were crazy. I went from extremely exhilarated to complete tragedy (in case you're living under a rock, I'm referring to AI coding)

item.title: How I Built an SMS Gateway with a $20 Android Phone
item.description: Turn any Android phone into a programmable SMS gateway for your SaaS — no per-message fees, no carrier contracts, no vendor lock-in.
item.author: (John Gregoriadis)

item.title: Thomas Gazagnaire :: A CSS Engine in OCaml
item.description: A typed CSS parser covering Level 3 through 5, a structural diff tool, and an optimiser. With a live browser demo via js_of_ocaml.

item.title: A Case for Procrastination
item.description: Or: why doing things at the last minute actually saves time. I talk about the importance of shockingly fast iteration cycles and lean manufacturing.

item.title: Oracle Files Thousands of H-1B Visa Petitions Amid Mass Layoffs - Austin Today
item.description: Oracle, the software company headquartered in Austin, Texas, has filed thousands of petitions for H-1B visas in the past two fiscal years, even as it lays off thousands of American workers as part of a broader organizational shift. Federal data shows Oracle filed for 2,690 H-1B visas in fiscal year 2025 and 436 so far in fiscal year 2026, totaling over 3,100 visa requests.

item.title: iNaturalist
item.description: iNaturalist is a social network for naturalists! Record your observations of plants and animals, share them with friends and researchers, and learn about the natural world.

item.title: blog | nworbmot:tombrown
item.description: First Posted: 2026.02.09, Last Revised: 2026.03.30, Author: Tom Brown

item.title: NHS staff refusing to use FDP over Palantir ethical concerns
item.description: A growing number of NHS staff are reportedly refusing to work on the Federated Data Platform (FDP) due to ethical concerns with its US-based provider, Palantir. The US technology company was...

item.title: Build your own Dial-up ISP with a Raspberry Pi
item.description: Last year my aunt let me add her original Tangerine iBook G3 clamshell to my collection of old Macs1. It came with an AirPort card—a $99 add-on Apple made that ushered in the Wi-Fi era. The iBook G3 was the first consumer laptop with built-in Wi-Fi antennas, and by far the cheapest way to get a computer onto an 802.11 wireless network.

item.title: apfel - Free AI on Your Mac
item.description: Use Apple's built-in AI from the terminal. No API keys, no cloud, no subscriptions. The LLM is already on your Mac.

item.title: European Alternatives to US Products & Software | Only EU
item.description: Find European alternatives for software, daily products, and tech. Stricter environmental standards, fair working conditions, and data sovereignty – Made in Europe.

item.title: Zipbombs are not as effective as they used to be
item.description: Last year, I wrote about my server setup and how I use zipbombs to mitigate attacks from rogue bots. It was an effective method that help my blog survive for 10 years. I usually hesitate to write thes

item.title: Day Counter
Programming (with AI agents) as theory building
item.description: Back in 1985, computer scientist Peter Naur wrote “Programming as Theory Building”. According to Naur - and I agree with him - the core output of software engineers is not the program itself, but the theory of how the program works. In other words, the knowledge inside the engineer’s mind is the primary artifact of engineering work, and the actual software is merely a by-product of that.
